items.title: Hayom Yom / daily Chitas (Chabad)
items.explanation: Chabad custom: daily Chumash, Tehillim, and Tanya (Chitas), plus Hayom Yom. Many complete it after morning prayers, but you may learn today's portion anytime before the next Hebrew day — including at night if you did not finish earlier.

items.title: Korbanot before Pesukei Dezimra (Ashkenaz)
items.explanation: Ashkenaz siddurim include Korbanot (Temple offerings) before Pesukei Dezimra. Many say at least Parshat HaTamid; follow your siddur and rabbi.

items.title: Korbanot & preliminaries (Nusach Ari)
items.explanation: Tehillat Hashem includes Korbanot and other passages before Pesukei Dezimra. Follow the order in your Chabad siddur.

items.title: Birkat Kohanim in Shacharit (Sephardi / Edot HaMizrach)
items.explanation: Sephardi and Edot HaMizrach communities in the Diaspora commonly recite Birkat Kohanim daily during Shacharit (customs vary — follow your kehilla and Shulchan Aruch O.C. 128).

items.title: Korbanot before Mincha (Sephardi)
items.explanation: Sephardi siddurim open Mincha with Korbanot — especially Parshat HaTamid (the daily Temple offering) — before Ashrei and the Amidah (Shulchan Arukh O.C. 1:5, 232:1). Follow your siddur and kehilla.

items.title: Korbanot before Mincha (Edot HaMizrach)
items.explanation: Edot HaMizrach siddurim open Mincha with Korbanot — especially Parshat HaTamid — before Ashrei and the Amidah, matching the Shulchan Arukh order (O.C. 1:5, 232:1). Some kehillot include additional passages; follow your siddur.

items.title: Tachanun at Shacharit (Ashkenaz weekdays)
items.explanation:
Tachanun at Shacharit (weekdays): After the morning Amidah — longer on Mondays and Thursdays (Vehu Rachum and the full penitential section), shorter on other weekdays. Omitted on Rosh Chodesh, festivals, Chanukah, and other days your siddur lists.

From Rosh Chodesh Sivan, many communities omit Tachanun until 12 Sivan — but some do say it; follow your minhag.

items.title: Tachanun at Shacharit (Sephardi weekdays)
items.explanation:
Tachanun at Shacharit (Sephardi weekdays): After the morning Amidah — Vidui, the Thirteen Attributes of Mercy, then Psalm 25 (LeDavid). Recite sitting upright per Rav Ovadia Yosef (Yechaveh Daat 6:7) and the Ben Ish Chai. Omitted on Rosh Chodesh, festivals, Chanukah, and other days your siddur lists.

From Rosh Chodesh Sivan, many communities omit Tachanun until 12 Sivan — but some do say it; follow your minhag.

items.title: Tachanun at Shacharit (Edot HaMizrach weekdays)
items.explanation:
Tachanun at Shacharit (Edot HaMizrach weekdays): After the morning Amidah — Vidui and the Thirteen Attributes per your siddur. Recite sitting upright per kabbalistic guidance cited by the Ben Ish Chai and Rav Ovadia Yosef. Omitted on Rosh Chodesh, festivals, Chanukah, and other days your siddur lists.

From Rosh Chodesh Sivan, many communities omit Tachanun until 12 Sivan — but some do say it; follow your minhag.

items.title: Tachanun at Shacharit (Chabad weekdays)
items.explanation:
Tachanun at Shacharit (Chabad weekdays): After the morning Amidah — longer on Mondays and Thursdays (Vehu Rachum), shorter on other days. Omitted on Rosh Chodesh, Yom Tov, Chanukah, and other special days your siddur lists.

From Rosh Chodesh Sivan, many communities omit Tachanun until 12 Sivan — but some do say it; follow your minhag.

items.title: Tachanun at Mincha (Ashkenaz weekdays)
items.explanation:
Tachanun at Mincha (weekdays): After the afternoon Amidah — on ordinary weekdays, typically the shorter form (often Vayomer David el Gad). Omitted on Rosh Chodesh, Yom Tov, Chanukah, and other days your siddur lists — Mincha itself is still said.

From Rosh Chodesh Sivan, many communities omit Tachanun until 12 Sivan — but some do say it; follow your minhag.

items.title: Tachanun at Mincha (Sephardi weekdays)
items.explanation:
Tachanun at Mincha (Sephardi weekdays): After the afternoon Amidah on ordinary weekdays. Recite sitting upright — Rav Ovadia Yosef (Yechaveh Daat 6:7) and the Ben Ish Chai rule against physical nefilat apayim at Mincha. Omitted on Rosh Chodesh, Yom Tov, Chanukah, Purim, and other festive days your siddur lists — Mincha itself is still said.

From Rosh Chodesh Sivan, many communities omit Tachanun until 12 Sivan — but some do say it; follow your minhag.

items.title: Tachanun at Mincha (Edot HaMizrach weekdays)
items.explanation:
Tachanun at Mincha (Edot HaMizrach weekdays): After the afternoon Amidah — including Vidui (Ashamnu) and the Thirteen Attributes per your siddur. Following the Arizal (per Kaf HaChaim and Yalkut Yosef), do not perform nefilat apayim at Mincha — recite sitting upright, including Psalm 25 (LeDavid Alecha). Omitted on Rosh Chodesh, holidays, and standard festive days — Mincha itself is still said.

From Rosh Chodesh Sivan, many communities omit Tachanun until 12 Sivan — but some do say it; follow your minhag.

items.title: Tachanun at Mincha (Chabad weekdays)
items.explanation:
Tachanun at Mincha (Chabad weekdays): After the afternoon Amidah — typically the shorter form (Vayomer David el Gad). Omitted on Rosh Chodesh, Yom Tov, Chanukah, Purim, and other special occasions your siddur lists — Mincha itself is still said.

From Rosh Chodesh Sivan, many communities omit Tachanun until 12 Sivan — but some do say it; follow your minhag.
